```jsx
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:

  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/components/ModalForm/ModalForm.tsx
2. Apply ONLY the change described by the migration rule at or near line 65
3. Make the minimum edit necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your change (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Write the fixed file
6. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through the fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edit, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists the change you made, or note if the fix was already applied or could not be applied (with a brief reason).
I'll start by reading the file to understand the full context.

Now let me analyze the migration needed:

**Incidents:**
1. **Line 65**: `title={title}` — must move to `<ModalHeader title={title} />`
2. **Line 67**: `showClose={false}` — removed prop. In v6, the close button is controlled by whether `onClose` is provided or by not including a close button in `<ModalHeader>`. Since `showClose={false}` means no close button, I should omit the close button behavior. In v6 Modal, the close button is part of `ModalHeader` — if we don't want a close button, we simply don't pass `onClose` to `ModalHeader` (but we still need `onClose` on `Modal` itself for escape key handling etc.).
3. **Line 71**: `actions={[...]}` — must move to `<ModalFooter>` as children
4. The `children` and error alert currently inside `<Modal>` should go inside `<ModalBody>`

Let me reason through the changes:

1. **Remove `title={title}`** from `<Modal>`, add `<ModalHeader title={title} />` as first child
2. **Remove `showClose={false}`** — In v6, `showClose` is removed. The close button in v6 is automatically included in `ModalHeader` if `onClose` is passed to the Modal. Since this code explicitly sets `showClose={false}`, we don't want a close button. Looking at PF v6 docs, the `ModalHeader` doesn't automatically show a close button — the close button behavior is now controlled differently. The simplest way to preserve the `showClose={false}` behavior is to just not render a close button explicitly.
3. **Remove `actions={[...]}`** from `<Modal>`, wrap the actions array in `<ModalFooter>` as children
4. **Wrap existing children in `<ModalBody>`**
5. **Add imports**: `ModalBody`, `ModalHeader`, `ModalFooter`

Let me also check for any test files:

No test files. Now let me apply the migration:

**Plan:**
1. Add `ModalBody`, `ModalHeader`, `ModalFooter` to the import from `@patternfly/react-core`
2. Remove `title={title}` prop from `<Modal>`
3. Remove `showClose={false}` prop from `<Modal>`
4. Remove `actions={[...]}` prop from `<Modal>`
5. Add `<ModalHeader title={title} />` as first child of `<Modal>`
6. Wrap existing `{children}` and error `<Alert>` in `<ModalBody>`
7. Add `<ModalFooter>` containing the actions array elements as children

For the `actions` array, in v5 it was an array of ReactNode passed as a prop. In v6, these become children of `<ModalFooter>`. The array elements can be placed directly as JSX children.

For `showClose={false}`: In PF v6, `ModalHeader` has an optional `onClose` callback that controls the close button. By not passing `onClose` to `ModalHeader`, no close button is shown — preserving the `showClose={false}` behavior. The `onClose` stays on `<Modal>` itself (for escape key / backdrop click).

arguments.after:
Button,
  type ButtonProps,
  ButtonVariant,
  Modal,
  ModalBody,
  ModalFooter,
  ModalHeader,
  ModalVariant,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';

content.text: Now replace the entire `<Modal>` JSX block:

arguments.after:
<Modal
      variant={variant}
      isOpen
      position="top"
      onClose={toggleModal}
      data-testid={testId}
      className={className}
    >
      <ModalHeader title={title} />
      <ModalBody>
        {children}

        {error && (
          <Alert title={t('Error')} variant={AlertVariant.danger} isInline>
            {t('{{errorMessage}}', { errorMessage: error })}
          </Alert>
        )}
      </ModalBody>
      <ModalFooter>
        <Button
          key="confirm"
          variant={confirmVariant ?? ButtonVariant.primary}
          onClick={handleConfirm}
          isLoading={isLoading}
          isDisabled={isLoading || Boolean(error) || isDisabled}
          data-testid="modal-confirm-button"
        >
          {confirmLabel ?? t('Save')}
        </Button>
        {additionalAction && (
          <Button key="secondary" {...additionalAction}>
            {additionalAction?.children}
          </Button>
        )}
        <Button
          key="cancel"
          variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
          onClick={toggleModal}
          data-testid="modal-cancel-button"
        >
          {cancelLabel ?? t('Cancel')}
        </Button>
      </ModalFooter>
    </Modal>
arguments.before:
<Modal
      variant={variant}
      title={title}
      isOpen
      showClose={false}
      position="top"
      onClose={toggleModal}
      data-testid={testId}
      actions={[
        <Button
          key="confirm"
          variant={confirmVariant ?? ButtonVariant.primary}
          onClick={handleConfirm}
          isLoading={isLoading}
          isDisabled={isLoading || Boolean(error) || isDisabled}
          data-testid="modal-confirm-button"
        >
          {confirmLabel ?? t('Save')}
        </Button>,
        ...(additionalAction
          ? [
              <Button key="secondary" {...additionalAction}>
                {additionalAction?.children}
              </Button>,
            ]
          : []),
        <Button
          key="cancel"
          variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
          onClick={toggleModal}
          data-testid="modal-cancel-button"
        >
          {cancelLabel ?? t('Cancel')}
        </Button>,
      ]}
      className={className}
    >
      {children}

      {error && (
        <Alert title={t('Error')} variant={AlertVariant.danger} isInline>
          {t('{{errorMessage}}', { errorMessage: error })}
        </Alert>
      )}
    </Modal>
